2603.17832.title: Text-to-Stage: Spatial Layouts from Long-form Narratives
2603.17832.paper_summary: In this work, we probe the ability of a language model to demonstrate spatial reasoning from unstructured text, mimicking human capabilities and automating a process that benefits many downstream media applications. Concretely, we study the narrative-to-play task: inferring stage-play layouts (scenes, speaker positions, movements, and room types) from text that lacks explicit spatial, positional, or relational cues. We then introduce a dramaturgy-inspired deterministic evaluation suite and, finally, a training and inference recipe that combines rejection SFT using Best-of-N sampling with RL from verifiable rewards via GRPO. Experiments on a text-only corpus of classical English literature demonstrate improvements over vanilla models across multiple metrics (character attribution, spatial plausibility, and movement economy), as well as alignment with an LLM-as-a-judge and subjective human preferences.
2603.17832.paper_summary_zh: 在這項工作中，我們探討了語言模型從非結構化文本中展示空間推理的能力，模仿人類的能力並自動化一個對許多下游媒體應用有利的過程。具體而言，我們研究了敘事到劇本的任務：從缺乏明確空間、位置或關係提示的文本中推斷舞台劇佈局（場景、講者位置、動作和房間類型）。然後，我們引入了一個受劇作法啟發的確定性評估套件，最後，一個結合使用最佳樣本的拒絕SFT和通過GRPO獲得可驗證獎勵的強化學習的訓練和推斷配方。對於純文本的古典英國文學語料庫的實驗顯示，在多個指標（角色歸屬、空間合理性和動作經濟）上，相較於普通模型有顯著改善，並且與作為評判者的LLM以及主觀人類偏好一致。
2603.17832.author: Jefferson Hernandez et.al.
2603.17832.authors: Jefferson Hernandez, Swarnadeep Saha, Chenxi Whitehouse, Sanjeel Parekh, Calvin Murdock, Yuliang Li, W. Owen Brimijoin, Vamsi Krishna Ithapu, Ishwarya Ananthabhotla
